title: Usage
text:
This section explains how to use the Air Analyzer project.


git clone https://github.com/AlessiaRinaldi/air_analyzer.git
cd air_analyzer



Open the firmware project inside the development environment and build it.

title: Air Analyzer
text:
Air Analyzer is an embedded system designed to monitor breathing-related activity using a microcontroller-based architecture.
The project focuses on sensor acquisition, firmware development, interrupt-based logic, and documentation of the embedded workflow.

title: Air Analyzer
text:
Air Analyzer is an embedded system designed to monitor breathing-related activity using a microcontroller-based architecture.
The project focuses on sensor acquisition, firmware development, interrupt-based logic, and documentation of the embedded workflow.

title: Overview
text:
Air Analyzer is a wearable embedded system for monitoring breathing-related activity.
The project is based on a microcontroller architecture and focuses on real-time acquisition, signal handling, and firmware organization.



Acquire data from the selected sensor.
Process the acquired signal on the microcontroller.
Detect useful information related to breathing activity.
Manage the firmware using an interrupt-based approach.
Provide clear and reproducible documentation.

title: Overview
text:

title: Firmware
text:
This section describes the embedded firmware of the Air Analyzer project.


The firmware is located mainly inside:
micro_usb/




Initialize peripherals
Acquire sensor data
Handle USB or serial communication
Process breathing-related signals
Manage interrupts or timers




The main application logic is located in:
micro_usb/Core/Src/main.c
